summary: The question of whether every $(a+b+2)$-edge-connected graph can have its edges partitioned into an $a$-edge-connected and a $b$-edge-connected spanning subgraph remains open. The only known partial result is via the Nash-Williams/Tutte theorem: if $G$ is $2(a+b)$-edge-connected, one can pack $a+b$ edge-disjoint spanning trees and assign $a$ to one part and $b$ to the other. No published paper appears to have closed the gap between $a+b+2$ and $2(a+b)$.

notes: The DJS paper (DeVos-Johnson-Seymour 'Cut-coloring and circuit covering') predates the 2007 posting date and is background, not new progress. The Princeton PDF (DJS) could not be parsed as text. The OPG page itself was unreachable (ECONNREFUSED). No arXiv or journal paper post-2007 specifically advancing the (a+b+2) partition conjecture was found across four search queries; the problem is likely still open but a definitive survey or MathSciNet search could confirm.
